subtitle: Clear inbox, multiple pending responses
context: Your inbox is clear as of Friday 19 June, but you have pending follow-ups awaiting responses from several colleagues. Michael O'Sullivan needs to contact Access Group regarding the unexpected case creation emails following new starter setup, a task you assigned on 18 June. Asta Palmer is running a comparison on Menu Profile for hierarchy administration that you requested on 15 June and should send details when complete. Laura Porter has confirmed additional contingency time for the IM Principles URL change that you discussed on 16 June. Marie Cooksey and Simon Burford are reviewing your Holiday Records Reporting proposal for FlexPoints Approval submitted 15 June. Your next critical deadline is the Clinical pay uplift 2026 check-in with Jasmin Khokhra on Monday 22 June at 15:00, where implementation progress will be reviewed. Open the Company 90 Test Approach meeting with Erika Braverman this morning at 09:45 first, as it addresses integration issues that may affect your weekend preparation.

prioritiesToday.text: Web pages and URL linking -- job posting updates
prioritiesToday.description: Internal Mobility Principles page moved to a new OxIntranet URL. Three pages still link to the old location: main ops intranet page, professional services search page, and individual vacancy application pages. New URL confirmed live on 3 June. Old page expected to be retired 23/24 June. Agreed approach: update all links to the new URL, use hyperlinked text ('IM Principles' / 'internal mobility principles') rather than displaying the full long URL, test before making any live changes, and check whether links can open in a new tab. Updates planned for 22 June ahead of old page retirement. Follow-up with Laura scheduled 15-16 June.
actions: [03 Jun 2026] Meeting with Laura Porter -- reviewed three pages needing link updates.
[03 Jun 2026] New URL confirmed live later that day: https://unioxfordnexus.sharepoint.com/sites/OXINTRANET-working-here/SitePages/internal-mobility-principles.aspx
[TODO -- today 16 Jun] Reconnect with Laura Porter as planned.
[TODO -- 22 Jun] Test new URL, update links on all three identified pages, replace visible long URLs with hyperlinked text, check whether links open in a new tab.
[TODO] Email Laura with updates if changes are made before they next meet.
[MONITOR] Old page retirement -- expected 23/24 June.

prioritiesToday.text: Pay Code 121 / PAYPAY25 -- add to HR Reporting SQL
prioritiesToday.description: Pay Code 121 (report reference PAYPAY25) needs to be added to the SQL in HR Reporting. Raised by Michael in 1-1 on 15 June -- needed for this month's payroll run. Process: identify which .RDL report(s) in the suite currently handle pay code filtering, edit the SQL to include PAYPAY25 / Pay Code 121, render in Visual Studio, and deploy through DEV -> TEST -> QA -> LIVE environments. KB reference: DRAFT_HOW TO - Manage Reports - Render a Report in Visual Studio; DRAFT_HOW TO - Manage Reports - Report Files.

prioritiesToday.text: Org hierarchy migration -- enable COREPORTAL_ADMIN menu options
prioritiesToday.description: As part of the Back Office to Portal org hierarchy migration (DTP1092 / college data input project), a set of menu options need to be enabled on COREPORTAL_ADMIN across all environments. Root cause confirmed by Michael O'Sullivan (17 Jun): the org structure management was NEVER migrated from Back Office into the Portal -- the college data input migration exposed this gap. No Portal-specific How-To guidance exists yet; documenting the process is a required follow-up action. Asta Palmer is running a comparison of what is currently enabled against what is needed and will send the full list to Kevin for the change request. Simon Burford identified the specific options needed in UOXU (two groups): HIERARCHY GROUP -- Show org Hierarchy, Delete hierarchy by business units, Show my hierarchy, Get hierarchy by business units, Set hierarchy items, Log/link hierarchy items, Set hierarchy levels, Update hierarchy by business units. POST MANAGEMENT GROUP -- Post Management, Post Appointment Maintenance, Post Management Parameters, Post Profile Maintenance, Post Restructuring, Post Profile Section History, Structure Setup, Terms and Conditions (Maintenance), View Post Appointments. Note from Michael: some functionality may be covered by COREPORTAL_SUPPORT or COREPORTAL_HR_ADMIN_WEB rather than COREPORTAL_ADMIN -- Asta's comparison will clarify. Kevin's approach: use UOXU as proof of concept before rolling out to remaining environments. Kevin will produce guidance and supporting documentation as part of this work. Helen's existing guide is the starting point: PeopleXD People Management - Maintain the Organisational Hierarchy v1 (Mar 2024), HR FA Knowledge Base.
actions: [15 Jun 2026] Action raised by Michael in 1-1 -- menu options to be enabled as part of Back Office to Portal org hierarchy migration. (email: Asta Palmer - RE: Menu Profile for hierarchy administration)

[12 Jun 2026] Asta Palmer confirmed Helen had been working on this; limitations prevented full migration to Portal at the time. Asta to run comparison of enabled vs required options on COREPORTAL_ADMIN and send to Kevin for the change request.

[17 Jun 2026] Michael O'Sullivan confirmed: org structure management was never migrated from Back Office to Portal -- that is why the options are not enabled. No Portal-specific How-To guidance documented; needs to be produced. Some functionality may sit under COREPORTAL_SUPPORT or COREPORTAL_HR_ADMIN_WEB.

[17 Jun 2026] Simon Burford confirmed the specific options he had to add in UOXU to get hierarchy access -- two groups identified (Hierarchy group and Post Management group -- see description for full lists).

[17 Jun 2026] Kevin replied to Asta: use UOXU as proof of concept before wider rollout. Kevin will put together guidance and supporting documentation to include as part of this work. Asked Asta to run comparison and send details.
[17 Jun 2026] Change request raised in OSM for COREPORTAL_ADMIN org hierarchy migration.
[AWAITING] Asta Palmer to run comparison and send full confirmed list of menu options to Kevin for the change request.
[TODO] Enable confirmed menu options on COREPORTAL_ADMIN in UOXU first (proof of concept), then DEV / TEST / QA / LIVE.
[TODO] Produce Portal-specific How-To guidance for org hierarchy management -- no documentation currently exists.
[TODO] Verify configuration in each environment before sign-off.
[TODO] Coordinate with Michael and Simon throughout.

[17 Jun 2026] Kevin asked Asta Palmer to run a comparison and send details of menu profile differences between environments on 15 June. (email: Kevin (sent to: Asta Palmer; Michael O'Sullivan; Simon Burford) - Re: Menu Profile for hierarchy administration)

prioritiesWeek.description: Smart notes in Cority CoreEHS+ (GX2) do not allow edits after save and close. Raised November 2025, dev ref CCC-19664. Cority's official position (confirmed 08 Jun 2026 by Mehran Riaz, support leadership): this is an INTENDED feature -- not a bug or missing feature. Cority classify the request to allow post-save edits as an enhancement, and it is NOT on their current roadmap. No timeline will be provided. Beta/early access ('enhanced access support') requires Premium Support, which Oxford does not have. Oxford has no named account manager -- we are in a pool. Escalation route: support case owner -> support leadership (done). If account/contract items needed, Cority sales team can be asked to reach out. Quality PUG on Tue 16 Jun at 2pm remains the formal pressure point.
actions: [Nov 2025] Ticket 00476764 raised by James -- smart notes cannot be edited after save/close.
[Nov-Jun] Multiple 'still working on it' updates from Freeda Franks (support analyst). Dev ref CCC-19664 assigned.
[08 Jun 2026] Kevin escalated formally -- requested timeline, clarification on cost comment, named account manager.

[08 Jun 2026] Mehran Riaz (support leadership) responded: feature is INTENDED behaviour, classified as enhancement, NOT on roadmap. No timeline. Premium Support required for beta access (Oxford does not have it). No named account manager -- pool model. Sales team available if needed.
[16 Jun 2026] Raised at Cority PUG with Christopher Sanders. PUG acknowledged smart notes as outstanding; Kevin to escalate directly with Cority for a firm delivery date.
[TODO] Chase Cority for firm delivery date on CCC-19664 draft/save functionality.
[TODO] Decide whether to pursue via sales/account route or accept as a known limitation and communicate to stakeholders.

prioritiesWeek.description: The shared drive was moved to SharePoint but guidance documentation was not updated to reflect the new location. Only Kevin and Lee were managing the migration, so other team members did not know where to find documents. The documents exist and are in the right place -- they are just not signposted correctly. Simon has raised this and Kevin needs to respond in writing before recommending an alignment meeting to bring the whole team up to speed. Michael also needs to be looped in.

[09 Jun 2026] Root cause confirmed at FA Team Catch-up: when content moved from shared drive to SharePoint/Teams, guidance pointing to documents was not updated. Created a single point of failure between Kevin and Lee -- others didn't know where to look. Documents exist and are correctly placed; signposting is the issue.

Two open HWP tickets are pending discussion with Gail Miller -- James has confirmed these can wait until he returns on 18 June.

prioritiesWeek.text: DSE data feed issues -- post go-live
prioritiesWeek.description: DSE data feed went live on Wednesday 3 June 2026. Three issues emerged immediately after go-live that require resolution. 1. SBS opted out of the main DSE system (they have their own) but were included in the initial feed -- Azure integrations team is handling their exclusion. 2. Contractors are missing from PeopleXD and therefore absent from the feed despite having DSE requirements -- a manual addition process is needed. 3. A small number of users do not have university email addresses, preventing standard access -- James is investigating with the supplier. James is on leave until 18 June. Kevin to monitor and flag any escalations. It was noted as disappointing that the SBS exclusion was a known requirement but was not built into the original feed.
actions: [03 Jun 2026] DSE data feed went live -- three issues identified post go-live.

[08 Jun 2026] Issues reviewed in H&S Roadmap meeting: SBS exclusion (Azure team handling), contractors missing from PXD feed (manual process needed), users without university emails (James investigating with supplier).
[MONITOR] SBS exclusion -- Azure integrations team handling. Confirm resolved when confirmed.
[AWAITING] James to return 18 June and progress contractor manual addition and email-less user resolution with supplier.
[TODO] Flag any escalations from Rob or first line while James is on leave.
